channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A bomb in Gaza's rubble wounds twins who thought it was a toy
item.description: GAZA CITY, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Shorbasi family was sitting in their severely damaged house in Gaza City, enjoying the relative calm of the ceasefire. Then they heard an explosion and rushed outside to find their 6-year-old twins bleeding on the ground.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Russian missile and drone attacks kill 4 in Ukraine as Zelenskyy pleads for air defense
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian missile and drone attacks on Ukraine overnight into Saturday killed at least four people and wounded 20, officials said, and prompted fresh pleas from Ukraine's president for Western air defense systems.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Company recalls 2 million pounds of barbecue pork jerky that may contain metal wire
item.description: (The Associated Press) -- A South Dakota company is recalling more than 2.2 million pounds (998,000 kilograms) of Korean barbecue pork jerky sold at Costco and Sam's Club stores because the product may be contaminated with pieces of metal, federal health officials said Friday. LSI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How the growing penny shortage may impact you
item.description: Several retailers have started to roll out policies as they run out of pennies. Here's what to expect if you're paying with cash.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Melodee Buzzard disappearance: New surveillance photos show missing California girl, 9, in possible disguise
item.description: Investigators in California released new images showing missing 9-year-old Melodee Buzzard wearing "what appears to be a wig" at a car rental business.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PHOTOS: Thieves cut through roof, steal hundreds of shoes in Florida mall heist
item.description: Florida authorities are searching for suspects who investigators said pulled off an elaborate heist to steal thousands of dollars in shoes from a Martin County mall this week.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US says it now plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia as soon as Oct. 31
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The U.S. government plans to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, and could do so as early as Oct. 31, according to a Friday court filing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Richard Mille watches worth 'hundreds of thousands of dollars' stolen during F1 weekend
item.description: “The group targeted wealthy individuals that were wearing high-end luxury watches,” court documents said.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: Task force says man to be deported 3rd time after arrest
item.description: The task force said he has an active order of removal from the country and will be deported for the third time.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, is pledging to embrace his Muslim identity amid growing attacks in the final days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: “In some instances, stopping your medicine may be more harmful to your health than continuing to take the recalled medicine,” the FDA says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Judge halts death penalty case in New Mexico due to federal shutdown
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A judge in New Mexico has halted a federal death penalty case, citing the pinch of the U.S. government shutdown and a funding shortfall for specialized public defenders that stretches back to the summer.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mafia returns to center stage in NBA betting scandal that included rigged poker games
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Decades after a crackdown by prosecutors decimated the ranks of the New York Mafia, the indictment of an NBA coach, a player and nearly three dozen others in a betting scandal highlighted the mob's persistence and adaptability to changing times and technology.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: Over 141,000 bottles of cholesterol medication have been recalled due to “failed dissolution specifications,” suggesting that the drugs did not pass a solubility test, or failed to dissolve at a standard rate.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Homeowner arrested after armed clash with teen prankster: report
item.description: Teen alleges Florida man forced him to knees at gunpoint over doorbell prank. Conflicting stories emerge as homeowner faces assault charges in Boca Raton incident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NBA head coach and player charged in sprawling sports betting and Mafia-backed poker schemes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers and a player for the Miami Heat were arrested Thursday along with more than 30 other people in a takedown of two sprawling gambling operations that authorities said leaked inside information about NBA athletes and rigged poker games backed
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, TIM REYNOLDS and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mother of missing California girl traveled with her out of state, returned without her
item.description: Melodee's mother, Ashlee Buzzard, has been uncooperative with the investigation, officials said, and has been unable to provide a satisfactory answer for her daughter's location or safety.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: States worry about how to fill the gap in food aid ahead of a federal benefits halt
item.description: Officials in Louisiana, Vermont and Virginia pledged Thursday to keep food aid flowing to recipients in their states, even if the federal program is stalled next month because of the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How Trump and Xi are doing a delicate, sometimes pointed dance in bilateral trade talks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The calm following the Sept. 19 phone call between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping had barely ended when strong undercurrents surged, threatening to rock the entire ship. First, the U.S. government widened sanction rules on Chinese companies.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and R.J. RICO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FBI announces arrests in federal sports gambling probe, including NBA figures
item.description: Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier and Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups have been arrested in connection with a federal investigation into sports betting, people familiar with the matter told The Associated Press on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and TIM REYNOLDS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mamdani, Cuomo and Sliwa clash bitterly during final NYC mayoral debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani was attacked over his thin resume, Republican Curtis Sliwa strove to prove his seriousness as a candidate and former Gov.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New US citizenship test proving difficult for immigrants
item.description: A new citizenship test that was rolled out this week by the Trump administration is being criticized as too difficult and could deter people from becoming U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: NYC woman says she lives in ‘constant fear’ after months-long ICE detention
item.description: “I lost three months of my life. I don’t want to [lose] my life again. I live in constant fear of being re-arrested,” Kostak said. “I don’t want to go back there.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WGN 9

item.title: Vendors on NYC's Canal Street say they were harassed and asked to show papers in immigration sweep
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A day after a mass of federal agents questioned street vendors and sparked protests on Manhattan's Canal Street, sellers were scarce on the busy strip. Some who did venture out Wednesday, though, were disheartened or riled up by a sweep in which they said people, including U.S.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 egg brands recalled for possible salmonella contamination
item.description: A recall issued last week has now expanded to include two brands of eggs that may be contaminated with salmonella.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: You didn't win: Mega Millions jackpot grows to $680 million
item.description: No one won big in Tuesday's drawing, and the Mega Millions jackpot has grown to an estimated $680 million.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: During cold and flu season, the youngest kids really are the germiest
item.description: Forget colorful leaves. Any caregiver knows that the real signs of fall are kids with coughs, sneezes and sniffles. Autumn marks the start of respiratory virus season, when colds, flu and other bugs start circulating — especially among the very young.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israeli settlers beat Palestinian farmers on video as attacks mount during West Bank olive harvest
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — Israeli settlers descended on Palestinian olive harvesters and activists this week in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, beating them with clubs in an attack Palestinian health officials said sent at least one woman to the hospital with serious injuries.
item.dc:creator: JALAL BWAITEL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Texas couple accused of moving, leaving child behind
item.description: A 12-year-old Texas boy came home from school to find it empty and his family gone, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Roland Richter, ABC 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors charge man accused of threatening to shoot up Atlanta airport
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Federal prosecutors on Tuesday brought charges against a Georgia man who was arrested a day earlier by police after his family contacted authorities saying he planned a shooting at Atlanta's international airport.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These are America’s safest – and most dangerous – cities, according to WalletHub
item.description: Threats ranging from public health and natural disasters to financial harm are among the many factors Americans face when they consider moving to a different city.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: US ranchers oppose Trump's plan to import more Argentine beef and experts doubt it will lower prices
item.description: SIOUX FALLS, S.D. (AP) — President Donald Trump ’s plan to cut record beef prices by importing more meat from Argentina is running into heated opposition from U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former NFL player who died in police custody battled injuries and mental health challenges
item.description: Doug Martin arrived on the campus of Boise State University nearly two decades ago to play football on its famed blue turf and, despite being a squat, little-known recruit from Northern California, turned himself into a hard-nosed running back and a first-round NFL draft pick.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and MARK LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A United Airlines emergency landing likely caused by collision with a weather balloon
item.description: A United Airlines plane appears to have collided with a weather balloon while cruising over Utah at 36,000 feet last week, fracturing layers of its windshield and forcing an emergency landing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil appears in appeals court as Trump administration continues efforts to deport him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mahmoud Khalil appeared Tuesday in a federal appeals court in Philadelphia as he continues to challenge a deportation case brought by President Donald Trump's administration over his pro-Palestinian activism at Columbia University.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One of the world's rarest whales that makes the Atlantic its home grows in population
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — One of the rarest whales on the planet has continued an encouraging trend of population growth in the wake of new efforts to protect the giant animals, according to scientists who study them.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4
